justification: Both models are equally poor: zero overlap with ground-truth WSe₂ features, no use of 2LA(M) enhancement or B¹₂g suppression, and confident misidentification of unrelated materials. Neither is closer to correct than the other.

step1.A: Completely incorrect; no WSe₂ identification, wrong material and band assignments
step1.B: Completely incorrect; no WSe₂ identification, wrong material and band assignments

step2.A: Not grounded in WSe₂ physics; hallucinations about graphite shear mode and overtones
step2.B: Not grounded in WSe₂ physics; focuses on HgS/Si and PL instead of TMD features

step3.A: No mention of WSe₂, monolayer indicators, or key evidence; irrelevant classification
step3.B: No mention of WSe₂, monolayer indicators, or key evidence; irrelevant classification

thinking: Both candidates completely failed to identify the spectrum as monolayer WSe₂. A misclassified it as bulk AB graphite (incorrectly labeling the ~249 cm⁻¹ peak as a shear mode and relying on D/G bands that are absent in clean WSe₂), while B misclassified it as cinnabar (HgS) plus Si (reasonable for the ~250/518 cm⁻¹ peaks but still wrong). Neither referenced E¹₂g/A₁g degeneracy, enhanced 2LA(M) at ~260 cm⁻¹, B¹₂g absence, or any TMD-specific diagnostics; their reasoning and outputs ignore all ground-truth criteria.
